@fabric_vc @wintermute_t ������

connecting an agent to a paid api today usually means five steps: sign up for an account, generate an api key, enter a credit card, configure the agent with those credentials, and then babysit usage so the bill doesn't blow up. every step in that flow assumes a human operator sitting in front of a dashboard.

agents follow a completely different operational rhythm than humans do. they spin up on demand, run for minutes or hours, and shut down when the task is done. expecting them to navigate signup forms and api key dashboards is asking them to be people.

the marketplace strips the human onboarding layer out of the equation entirely. an agent finds the service, pays per request from its own wallet, and moves on. the model finally matches how agents actually run.

browse the ampersend marketplace ⬇️

RT @leighcronian: People occasionally ask why I continue building around @virtuals_io and @base

The answer is actually pretty simple.

Int…
RT @everythingempty: skin in the game https://t.co/KlFI83hvpz

I made $6,300 on twitter. Here is what I learned

I got curious so I fully analyzed my twitter profile going back 6 years

I one-shot tweets. Ideas pop into my mind and I tweet them. No second guesses. No optimization bullshit (full report below)

I use the word "fucking" a lot. People like the authenticity I guess. Maybe it signals financial independence or something.

As you can see from all the grammar and spelling mistakes I never use AI. if you use AI to write your tweets your should be put in twitter jail.

A lot of tech. A lot of crypto. This is what I know the most about. Also the hiring stuff goes viral. No clue why.

Never tweeted something I don't believe. As you can imagine I get offered sponsorship stuff all the time. I'm not interested.

Also get asked a lot to "amplify" things. I never do it if I don't like the content. Kinda awkward sometimes but it is what it is.
People also ask to be followed back. That's just cringe bro.

My pfp is kinda known at this point. Yes I treat it as a brand. I would never change it.

I like to "monkey brain" things. Maybe I'm good at it because english is not my first language. Just say something in the most clear way possible (definitely copied from naval). Keep it super short.

I care about how my profile looks like. You will never see me retweet or quote tweet things a lot. I think it kinda looks ugly. I also go back and delete things. I don't think it actually matters. Just a me thing

Friends send me tweets that are direct copies of my stuff all the time. I take it as a compliment hahaha

You can go back 6 years and I was tweeting in the same format. Kinda funny actually. Same format just a bigger audience.

I always tell people to post about things they are building on X. no downside and huge upside. its literally a cheat code.

Yes my twitter profile opened up some doors. Dms get answered more frequently. You get invited to more stuff. It's true and I don't even have a big profile.

Also dont tweet to make money. it does not work like that. I never did and never will. I just thought people will find this interesting.

ohh and I'm definitely gonna use this to shill what we are building because its fucking sick haha (it created the whole report)
